name: Metamaterial Antenna
description: An antenna that incorporates metamaterial structures with engineered electromagnetic properties not found in natural materials, typically exhibiting negative permittivity or permeability. These antennas can achieve enhanced directivity, bandwidth, or miniaturization through the manipulation of electromagnetic wave propagation. Metamaterial antennas enable novel functionalities such as beam steering, cloaking, and super-resolution imaging.

unit_cell_size.name: Unit Cell Size
unit_cell_size.description: Physical dimension of the metamaterial unit cell, typically much smaller than wavelength

relative_permittivity.name: Effective Relative Permittivity
relative_permittivity.description: Effective permittivity of the metamaterial structure, can be negative

relative_permeability.name: Effective Relative Permeability
relative_permeability.description: Effective permeability of the metamaterial structure, can be negative

substrate_thickness.name: Substrate Thickness
substrate_thickness.description: Thickness of the dielectric substrate supporting the metamaterial structure

number_of_layers.name: Number of Metamaterial Layers
number_of_layers.description: Number of metamaterial layers in the antenna structure

designMethodology.overview: Metamaterial antenna design involves creating periodic structures with engineered electromagnetic properties, followed by integration with conventional radiating elements to achieve desired antenna characteristics.

steps.title: Define Target Properties
steps.description: Specify desired effective permittivity and permeability values based on application requirements

steps.title: Unit Cell Design
steps.description: Design metamaterial unit cell geometry (split-ring resonators, wire arrays, etc.) to achieve target properties

steps.title: Parameter Extraction
steps.description: Extract effective material parameters using S-parameter retrieval methods
steps.formula: epsilon_eff = (n^2 - z^2) / z^2, mu_eff = n * z

steps.title: Antenna Integration
steps.description: Integrate metamaterial structure with radiating element (patch, dipole, etc.)

steps.title: Impedance Matching
steps.description: Design matching network to achieve 50-ohm input impedance

steps.title: Performance Optimization
steps.description: Optimize unit cell dimensions and spacing for desired gain and bandwidth

equations.inputImpedance: Z_in = sqrt(mu_eff / epsilon_eff) * Z_0 * tanh(gamma * h)
equations.radiationPattern: E(theta, phi) = E_0 * F_element(theta, phi) * F_array(theta, phi) * F_metamaterial(theta, phi)

keyAssumptions: Periodic boundary conditions for unit cell analysis
Losses included through imaginary parts of material parameters

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 21
Caloz, C. and Itoh, T. 'Electromagnetic Metamaterials: Transmission Line Theory and Microwave Applications'
Volakis, J.L. 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 42
Engheta, N. and Ziolkowski, R.W. 'Metamaterials: Physics and Engineering Explorations'
